The chances of an outright increase are therefore minimal, rising marginally by February due only to a small degree of forecasting horizon uncertainty.

The most recent value of the national average savings account interest rate (SNDR) was 0.400. In late October 2025, the Federal Reserve cut the federal funds rate by 0.25 percentage points (its second consecutive 25 bps cut). Savings deposit rates, particularly high-yield savings accounts which heavily influence the weighted average, tend to decrease in response to Fed rate cuts. Because of this strong downward pressure, the probability that the national average savings rate will strictly *increase* from its current 0.40% by December or February is very low. The rate may remain flat (as it did between September and November) or decrease. The probability of an increase is largely driven by random fluctuations in deposit weighting (which caused a tiny bounce in mid-2025), but the macro environment is strongly biased against an increase. I place the probability for the first resolution date low, as it may just capture the November or early December reading (likely flat or lower), and slightly higher for the second date to reflect increased uncertainty over a longer horizon.

The value on November 9, 2025, corresponds to the post-meeting level following the Fed's October 29 rate cut (dropping from ~4.31 to an immediate floor around 3.91, then settling at 3.93 by Nov 7). Since there are no FOMC meetings scheduled for November, the target rate will remain strictly unchanged for the Nov 16 and Dec 9 resolution dates. Daily repo market pressures often revert toward the IORB (roughly 3.90-3.92 for the new 3.75-4.00% target range). Thus, 3.93 represents a slightly elevated level within this band, meaning that near-term observations on Nov 16 and Dec 9 face natural downward regression toward the 3.91 mean, keeping the likelihood of printing *strictly greater* than 3.93 somewhat below 50% (roughly 45% and 35%, accounting for mid-month and mid-cycle repo variations). By the Feb 07, 2026 date, the Fed will have concluded its December and January FOMC meetings. Current market pricing via the CME FedWatch Tool implies a ~64% likelihood of a 25 bps cut at the December meeting alone, and overall expectations heavily skew toward at least one cut by February. In that scenario, SOFR will drop to the ~3.65-3.70% neighborhood, virtually eliminating the chance of exceeding 3.93. Factoring in a small probability of the Fed pausing its rate cuts due to persistent inflation gives us an estimated 15% chance of the rate being elevated by February.

Sepsis is a clinical syndrome triggered by a dysregulated host response to a wide variety of pathogens (bacterial, viral, fungal), rather than a single specific infectious agent. Because of its massive etiological diversity, developing a universal "sepsis vaccine" is scientifically extremely difficult, if not purely impossible. While vaccines exist for specific pathogens that can lead to sepsis (such as pneumococcus), there is no unified vaccine for "sepsis" itself. The Wikipedia page "List of infectious diseases" correctly listed the Sepsis vaccine status as "No" on October 30, 2025. There are no late-stage clinical trials for anything resembling a universal sepsis vaccine that could be approved and added to Wikipedia between November 2025 and February 2026. Therefore, the probability of the status changing to "Yes" on Wikipedia is functionally zero, except for the marginal baseline risk of Wikipedia vandalism or an unusual categorical redefinition by Wikipedia editors. Assigning near the minimum allowed probabilities, slightly increasing over time to reflect the growing (but still marginal) risk of unexpected wiki edits over longer horizons.

Bobby Finke holds the fully ratified world record in the 1500m long course freestyle, set in August 2024 with a time of 14:30.67 (the Wikipedia table up to Nov 2025 shows no '#' indicating pending ratification). The 2025 World Championships concluded in early August 2025, with the gold medalist swimming 14:34.41—significantly slower than Finke's record. World records in this event tend to be long-lasting (Sun Yang held the previous record for 12 years, and Grant Hackett before him for a decade). Furthermore, the upcoming resolution dates (November 2025 through February 2026) fall primarily during the short course season in swimming, making the likelihood of top competitors peaking for a long course 1500m race extremely small. There are no credible doping suspensions or controversies involving Finke that would suggest an imminent retroactive annulment. Consequently, retaining the record across these dates is highly probable, with uncertainty marginally increasing for the latest date due to unexpected contingencies.

The 200m butterfly long course world record has been held by Liu Zige since October 2009 (2:01.81). As confirmed by the Wikipedia revision as of November 4, 2025, this record remains standing. The only active swimmer who has come close is Summer McIntosh, who swam a 2:01.99 in July 2025, just 0.18 seconds off the record. Regan Smith is the next closest active competitor but is about three seconds behind. Consequently, the record is only realistically threatened by McIntosh. The primary timeframe under consideration falls between November 2025 and February 2026. This period is historically the short-course season for elite swimming, headlined by the Short Course World Championships in December 2025. While there are some long course meets during this window—such as the US Open (Dec 3-6) and early Pro Swim Series events in early 2026—these are mid-season training meets where elite swimmers do not typically taper, making world records exceedingly rare. Given that the closest taper events (like national trials) usually occur from March onward, the probability of the record falling before February 2026 is extremely low. Thus, the confidence that Liu Zige will remain the record holder remains exceptionally high across all three resolution dates, slightly decreasing over time to account for the expanding 'at-risk' events window.

As of late 2025, there is no vaccine for Chancroid (caused by Haemophilus ducreyi). The most recent scientific literature from 2025 refers to surface proteins only as "potential targets" for the development of vaccines, indicating that no vaccine is currently in late-stage clinical trials or nearing approval. Furthermore, the global prevalence of the disease has decreased significantly, making rapid, "Warp Speed"-style development highly improbable. The process of developing, approving, and publicly listing a new vaccine takes years, far exceeding the 1 to 3-month forecast horizon considered here. Therefore, the probability that Wikipedia will be updated to reflect a newly developed vaccine by any of the resolution dates is extremely low. I am setting the probabilities to the minimum allowable range (0.05), with a negligible increase over the 3-month horizon to account for unforeseen developments.

The target requires Hikaru Nakamura to reach an Elo rating 1% higher than his Nov 9, 2025 baseline (which is 2813). A 1% increase equates to gaining approximately 28 Elo points, putting his target rating around 2841. 

1. **2025-11-16**: FIDE ratings are updated monthly on the 1st of the month. The Wikipedia page will display the exact same November 1 rating list on Nov 16 as it did on Nov 9. Therefore, an increase is impossible. P = 0.02.
2. **2025-12-09**: To gain 28 points in a single month at a 2813 rating (K-factor = 10, World #2), Nakamura would need to outperform his expected score by 2.8 points. In standard elite tournaments, to achieve this (+5 or +6 score over expected) is virtually impossible over the course of a single event. Furthermore, FIDE recently closed a rule loophole allowing 2650+ players to easily 'farm' points against much lower-rated opponents. Given his target is simply protecting his 2813 rating to qualify for the 2026 Candidates Tournament, he will minimize risk. P = 0.03.
3. **2026-02-07**: Achieving a 2841 rating would far surpass Nakamura's historical peak of 2816, moving him into the top 3 highest peak classical ratings in chess history. Given that he is a 37-year-old taking deliberate, safe steps to secure a Candidates spot, rather than risking massive fluctuations, reaching 2841 by February 2026 is exceptionally unlikely. P = 0.04.

The table on Wikipedia's 'List of infectious diseases' does not currently list any vaccines for Gonorrhea. The only major development is the off-label use of the Meningitis B vaccine (Bexsero/4CMenB) in some regions (like England) for its partial cross-protection. However, this has been well known for a year or more without prompting Wikipedia editors to classify it as a Gonorrhea vaccine in this specific summary table. Dedicated vaccines, such as GSK's NgG candidate, are only in Phase 1/2 clinical trials and will absolutely not reach approval by early 2026. Therefore, the only path to resolution is a random, unprompted Wikipedia edit to add the off-label MenB vaccine to the table, which has a very low base rate of occurring. Probabilities are kept very low, increasing slightly over time to account for cumulative random edit risk.

For the first date (Nov 16, 2025), FIDE rankings update strictly on the 1st of every month, meaning Wikipedia's November Top 100 list will remain identical to the Nov 9 list (p=0.97). As of November 1, Wesley So is ranked #8 (2764). However, the rating gap is highly asymmetric: he is 4 points behind #7 Praggnanandhaa, but only 1 point ahead of #9 Gukesh (2763) and 2 points ahead of #10 Firouzja (2762). Because of this narrow margin and the presence of highly active young players directly beneath him (such as Gukesh playing the World Championship in late November), So is more likely to be overtaken than to advance. Consequently, for December 9, I estimate a 43% chance he maintains a rank of 8 or better. For February 7, as the forecast horizon extends and uncertainty naturally increases, the estimate reverts slightly toward the baseline of 0.47.

Therefore, the probability remains low, increasing only marginally to reflect uncertainty over a slightly longer horizon.

According to the November 1, 2025 FIDE rankings on Wikipedia, Magnus Carlsen is ranked #1 with a classical rating of 2839, holding a massive 26-point lead over #2 Hikaru Nakamura (2813). FIDE rankings update monthly, meaning the November 16 resolution date will perfectly align with the current November 1 list, heavily guaranteeing he remains #1 (barring Wikipedia vandalism or unprecedented mid-month adjustments). For the December 9 list (which reflects December 1 updates), the primary classical event in November is the 2025 FIDE World Cup. However, Carlsen, Nakamura, Caruana, and several other top players have withdrawn from it, meaning their classical ratings will effectively be frozen for the December list. For the final resolution date of February 7, 2026, rankings will incorporate results from any late December or January events (like Tata Steel Chess). To overtake Carlsen, a competitor would need to bridge a 26-point gap in one or two classical tournaments, an overwhelmingly difficult feat for a 2800+ player since draws against lower-rated opponents cause rating loss. Carlsen's most recent major classical event was Norway Chess in June 2025, and since a player only loses active status after 12 months with no classical rated games, he is safely immune from removal by February 2026. Therefore, Carlsen is exceedingly likely to retain the #1 ranking on all three dates.

The Elo rating is updated monthly by FIDE and Wikipedia copies this list. The November 1 list has Shakhriyar Mamedyarov at 2742. On November 9, his rating on Wikipedia will be 2742, and on November 16 it will still be the exact same November list (2742). Therefore, the probability of it being exactly 1% higher (~2770) on Nov 16 is virtually zero. For the Dec 9 and Feb 7 dates, he would need to gain roughly 28 Elo points. Given his K-factor of 10, this requires scoring roughly +2.8 expected points over his performance in upcoming events like the FIDE World Cup (i.e. an incredible streak of dominant wins over other grandmasters without draws). For a 40-year-old super GM, a +28 point rating surge in 1 to 3 months is exceedingly rare. Many top GMs actually lose standard rating points in knockout events like the World Cup due to draws against lower-rated opponents. Thus, the chances remain exceedingly slim across all dates.

There are no human vaccines for Pasteurellosis available or in late-stage clinical trials. While animal vaccines exist (e.g., for cattle and poultry), the Wikipedia article "List of infectious diseases" correctly listed the vaccine status as "No" as of late October 2025, showing that animal vaccines are not considered for this cell. Therefore, for the resolution to change to "Yes" on any of the target dates, either an unprecedentedly rapid human vaccine development would need to occur (which is practically impossible in this 1–3 month timeframe), or a Wikipedia editor would have to mistakenly add an animal vaccine (or vandalize the page) and the edit would have to survive without being reverted by the resolution date. Given the high visibility of medical articles on Wikipedia, such errors are typically reverted quickly. The probability is set to the minimum allowed value of 0.05 for the first date, slightly increasing for later dates to reflect the minimally growing risk of an uncorrected erroneous edit over time.

To evaluate this question, we must estimate the probability that Wei Yi’s FIDE Elo rating, as listed on the Wikipedia 'FIDE rankings' page, will be at least 1% higher than his rating on November 9, 2025.
1. The Wikipedia page reflects the FIDE monthly ratings. On Nov 1, 2025, Wei Yi's rating was 2753. This value will also be the rating on Nov 9, 2025, as FIDE updates classical ratings monthly on the 1st.
2. An increase of 1% equates to gaining ~28 Elo points, requiring Wei Yi to reach 2781. 
3. For November 16, 2025: The Wikipedia page will predictably not have changed its underlying December data since the FIDE list only drops on the 1st of every month. The rating will be identical (2753), rendering the probability effectively 0 (capped at the 0.05 minimum).
4. For December 9, 2025: This falls under the December 1st FIDE list. To gain 28 points in one rating period, Wei Yi would have needed a historic performance at the FIDE World Cup 2025 (e.g., scoring +6 or +7 wins against Elite GMs without a loss in Classical format). Such a surge at the top level is extremely rare, especially since matches often go to rapid tiebreaks (which don't impact Classical Elo).
5. For February 7, 2026: Wei Yi has until the February FIDE list (allowing participation in other major winter events, like Tata Steel) to accumulate these 28 points. While possible over a broader horizon, achieving an Elo of 2781 (+28 points) represents passing his peak rating by a considerable margin. Our uncertainty marginally widens over this longer timeline.

Developing a vaccine for Enterobiasis (pinworm) is scientifically difficult given it is a multicellular parasite, and there is almost no commercial or public health incentive to do so because the infection is very mild and easily curable with cheap, highly effective oral medications (e.g., albendazole, pyrantel pamoate). Current literature shows no clinical trials for any pinworm vaccine. Furthermore, the Wikipedia "List of infectious diseases" page lists no vaccine for Enterobiasis as of late 2025. Given timelines for vaccine development, the probability of a functional vaccine being created, verified, and listed on Wikipedia within the next three to six months is virtually zero. The minimal probabilities assigned (0.05, 0.06, 0.07) only reflect the background risk of Wikipedia vandalism or definition changes over longer time horizons.

Based on recent information, there is currently no approved vaccine for Calicivirus (Norovirus and Sapovirus) as of late 2025. HilleVax's vaccine failed a mid-stage trial in July 2024. Moderna's Phase 3 vaccine trial for Norovirus experienced delays due to a lack of cases, pushing its case accumulation into the 2025-2026 Northern Hemisphere season; efficacy data won't be ready until 2026, and certainly no approval will be granted before the final resolution date of February 2026. Vaxart is only moving into Phase 2b in 2025. Given the lengthy regulatory review process (usually 6-12 months post Phase 3), there is virtually no chance a Norovirus vaccine will be developed and approved by February 2026. Setting the probability to the minimum allowed value for all dates.

The stock IPG is heavily constrained by its ongoing acquisition by Omnicom (OMC) in a stock-for-stock transaction (0.344 exchange ratio). The transaction is in its final phase, awaiting only European Commission regulatory approval around November 24, 2025. Between November 9 and November 16, IPG is expected to file its Q3 2025 10-Q (on or before November 10). Because IPG's price is primarily pegged to OMC's stock price and the remaining merger spread, standalone Q3 earnings volatility will likely be muted unless there is a catastrophic miss that threatens a 'material adverse change' (MAC) clause, which is exceptionally rare. Omnicom already reported strong Q3 earnings on October 21, affirming its commitment to the deal. Any natural drift over this 1-week window will be dominated by standard market base rates (~53% for 1-week positive returns) plus a slight upward bias from merger spread compression as the final closing date approaches. There is also a small chance the EU approves the merger early within this window, which would close the spread entirely and pop the stock up slightly. Taken together, the probability leans slightly positive relative to a pure coin toss.
